steps.title: Recognize the exterior angle configuration
steps.content: When two sides of a triangle are extended, they form exterior angles outside the triangle. The angle x is one such exterior angle.

steps.title: Apply the exterior angle theorem and compute x
steps.content:
An exterior angle equals the sum of the two non-adjacent interior angles. From the figure, the two remote interior angles measure 50° and 70°, so:
$$x = 50° + 70° = 120°$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
